---
title: "Nasal Swab Cell Isolation for scRNA-seq Livestream"
description: "Ordovas-Montanes Lab, Boston Children's Hospital"
# date: "2024-10-15"
# categories: [Wet Lab]
format:
  html:
    includes:
      in_header: style.css
      before_body: cover.html
---

<script type="text/javascript">
document.write(`
<!-- Slideshow and text section for illustration -->
<div id="intro-section" style="position: relative; text-align: center; color: white;">

  <!-- Slideshow Background with images, text, and heading -->
  <div class="slideshow-container">
    
    <!-- Slide 1 -->
    <div class="mySlides fade">
      <div style="background-image: url('../../files/img/training/Nasal_Swab_Cell_Isolation_for_scRNA_seq/NSP_0.jpg'); background-size: cover; background-position: center; height: 100vh;">
        <div style="position: absolute; bottom: 15%; left: 50%; transform: translateX(-50%); text-align: center; color: white;">
          <h1 style="font-size: 2.5em; font-weight: bold;">Cell Isolation from Nasopharyngeal Swabs</h1>
          <p style="font-size: 1.3em; max-width: 800px;">
            This protocol provides step-by-step instructions to isolate cells from frozen nasopharyngeal swabs. A typical yield of 50,000 to 100,000 cells can be obtained per swab. This protocol was demonstrated live and followed up with library preparation and sequencing on NextSeq 2000.
          </p>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>

    <!-- Slide 2 -->
    <div class="mySlides fade">
      <div style="background-image: url('../../files/img/training/Nasal_Swab_Cell_Isolation_for_scRNA_seq/NSP_1.png'); background-size: cover; background-position: center; height: 100vh;">
        <div style="position: absolute; bottom: 15%; left: 50%; transform: translateX(-50%); text-align: center; color: white;">
          <h1 style="font-size: 2.5em; font-weight: bold;">Materials and Reagents</h1>
          <p style="font-size: 1.3em; max-width: 800px;">
            To isolate cells, you will need: RPMI, DTT, Accutase, quenching buffer (RPMI + 10% FBS + 4 mM EDTA), sterile tubes, and a variety of lab equipment. Ensure the swabs are cryopreserved in FBS + DMSO and stored at -80°C or LN2 before processing.
          </p>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>

    <!-- Slide 3 -->
    <div class="mySlides fade">
      <div style="background-image: url('../../files/img/training/Nasal_Swab_Cell_Isolation_for_scRNA_seq/NSP_2.png'); background-size: cover; background-position: center; height: 100vh;">
        <div style="position: absolute; bottom: 15%; left: 50%; transform: translateX(-50%); text-align: center; color: white;">
          <h1 style="font-size: 2.5em; font-weight: bold;">Procedure Overview</h1>
          <p style="font-size: 1.3em; max-width: 800px;">
            Key steps include rapidly thawing the swab, cell dissociation with Accutase and RPMI, filtering cells through a 70 µm filter, and counting viable cells with trypan blue before downstream processing.
          </p>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>

    <!-- Slide 4 -->
    <div class="mySlides fade">
      <div style="background-image: url('../../files/img/training/Nasal_Swab_Cell_Isolation_for_scRNA_seq/NSP_3.png'); background-size: cover; background-position: center; height: 100vh;">
        <div style="position: absolute; bottom: 15%; left: 50%; transform: translateX(-50%); text-align: center; color: white;">
          <h1 style="font-size: 2.5em; font-weight: bold;">Cell Counting and Preparation for scRNA-seq</h1>
          <p style="font-size: 1.3em; max-width: 800px;">
            After cell counting, proceed with library preparation for scRNA-seq. This involves using the 10X Genomics platform to capture individual cells and prepare them for sequencing.
          </p>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>

    <!-- Slide 5 -->
    <div class="mySlides fade">
      <div style="background-image: url('../../files/img/training/Nasal_Swab_Cell_Isolation_for_scRNA_seq/NSP_4.png'); background-size: cover; background-position: center; height: 100vh;">
        <div style="position: absolute; bottom: 15%; left: 50%; transform: translateX(-50%); text-align: center; color: white;">
          <h1 style="font-size: 2.5em; font-weight: bold;">Post-Isolation Cell Processing</h1>
          <p style="font-size: 1.3em; max-width: 800px;">
            Proceed to cell staining or downstream applications such as single-cell RNA sequencing to assess the transcriptomic profile of the isolated cells.
          </p>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>

    <!-- Slide 6 -->
    <div class="mySlides fade">
      <div style="background-image: url('../../files/img/training/Nasal_Swab_Cell_Isolation_for_scRNA_seq/NSP_5.png'); background-size: cover; background-position: center; height: 100vh;">
        <div style="position: absolute; bottom: 15%; left: 50%; transform: translateX(-50%); text-align: center; color: white;">
          <h1 style="font-size: 2.5em; font-weight: bold;">Equipment Overview</h1>
          <p style="font-size: 1.3em; max-width: 800px;">
            Equipment includes a thermomixer, hemocytometer slides, and standard lab equipment such as forceps, scissors, and sterile pipettes.
          </p>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>

    <!-- Slide 7 -->
    <div class="mySlides fade">
      <div style="background-image: url('../../files/img/training/Nasal_Swab_Cell_Isolation_for_scRNA_seq/NSP_6.png'); background-size: cover; background-position: center; height: 100vh;">
        <div style="position: absolute; bottom: 15%; left: 50%; transform: translateX(-50%); text-align: center; color: white;">
          <h1 style="font-size: 2.5em; font-weight: bold;">Results and Yield</h1>
          <p style="font-size: 1.3em; max-width: 800px;">
            The cell isolation process typically yields between 50,000 to 100,000 cells per swab. Results vary depending on sample quality and processing conditions.
          </p>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>

    <!-- Slide 8 -->
    <div class="mySlides fade">
      <div style="background-image: url('../../files/img/training/Nasal_Swab_Cell_Isolation_for_scRNA_seq/NSP_7.png'); background-size: cover; background-position: center; height: 100vh;">
        <div style="position: absolute; bottom: 15%; left: 50%; transform: translateX(-50%); text-align: center; color: white;">
          <h1 style="font-size: 2.5em; font-weight: bold;">Collaboration and Partners</h1>
          <p style="font-size: 1.3em; max-width: 800px;">
            This protocol is developed in collaboration with Boston Children's Hospital, MIT, and the Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation to study respiratory diseases using cutting-edge single-cell RNA sequencing techniques.
          </p>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>

    <!-- Video slide -->
    <div class="mySlides fade">
      <div style="text-align: center; background-color: black; height: 100vh;">
        <video width="800" controls style="margin-top: 150px;">
          <source src="../../files/img/training/Nasal_Swab_Cell_Isolation_for_scRNA_seq/thai.mp4" type="video/mp4">
          Your browser does not support the video tag.
        </video>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>

<!-- Structured Program Details Below the Slideshow -->
<div id="protocol-details" style="padding: 20px; background-color: #f9f9f9; max-width: 21cm; margin: 0 auto;">

  <h2 style="text-align: center; font-weight: bold;">Materials & Equipment</h2>
  <ul style="font-size: 1.2em; max-width: 800px; margin: 0 auto; text-align: left;">
    <li>RPMI + 10 mM dithiothreitol (DTT) (freshly prepared)</li>
    <li>Accutase (3.5 mL per swab)</li>
    <li>Quenching buffer: RPMI + 10% fetal bovine serum (FBS) + 4 mM EDTA</li>
    <li>50 mL conical tubes, 15 mL conical tubes, and 1.5 mL eppendorf tubes</li>
    <li>Thermomixer set to 37°C, agitating at 300 rpm</li>
    <li>Hemocytometer slides</li>
    <li>Forceps, scissors, and sterile pipettes</li>
    <li>Optional: 10X Genomics reagents for single-cell capture</li>
  </ul>

  <h3 style="text-align: center; font-weight: bold;">Procedure Steps</h3>
  <div style="font-size: 1.2em; max-width: 800px; margin: 0 auto; text-align: left;">
    <strong>Step 1: Thaw and prepare samples</strong><br>
    Rapidly thaw the cryopreserved swab in a thermal block set to 37°C. Rinse the swab briefly in RPMI, then transfer it to Tube C containing Accutase.<br><br>

    <strong>Step 2: Dissociate cells</strong><br>
    Incubate the swab in Accutase for 30 minutes at 37°C with shaking at 300 rpm. After incubation, pass the cells through a 70 µm filter and centrifuge to collect cells.<br><br>

    <strong>Step 3: Count cells</strong><br>
    Resuspend the pellet in RPMI + 10% FBS and use trypan blue to count viable cells using a hemocytometer.<br><br>

    <strong>Step 4: Prepare for single-cell capture</strong><br>
    After cell counting, proceed to 10X Genomics single-cell capture if performing scRNA-seq. Ensure all reagents are equilibrated to room temperature before proceeding with the capture step.
  </div>

  <h3 style="text-align: center; font-weight: bold;">Next Steps and Analysis</h3>
  <div style="font-size: 1.2em; max-width: 800px; margin: 0 auto; text-align: left;">
    <p>After cell isolation, proceed with single-cell RNA sequencing library preparation, followed by sequencing on the NextSeq 2000 platform. The computational analysis can be performed using tools like Seurat and Scanpy.</p>
    <p>This protocol was developed in collaboration with MIT, Boston Children's Hospital, and the Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation.</p>
  </div>
</div>
`);
</script>

<style>
  #program-details {
    font-family: Arial, sans-serif;
    color: #333;
    line-height: 1.6;
    padding: 40px;
    background-color: #f9f9f9;
    max-width: 1800px; /* Increased width */
    margin: 0 auto; /* Center the content */
  }

  h2, h3 {
    margin-top: 50px;
    margin-bottom: 30px;
    font-weight: bold;
    text-align: center;
  }

  div {
    font-size: 1em;
    margin: 0 auto;
    max-width: 1400px; /* Increased width for text */
    text-align: center;
  }

  ul, p {
    font-size: 1.3em; /* Larger text size for better readability */
    line-height: 1.8; /* Increased line-height for better readability */
    max-width: 1400px; /* Increased width for better alignment with images */
    margin: 0 auto;
    text-align: left; /* Align information content to the left */
  }

  /* Slideshow container */
  .slideshow-container {
    position: relative;
    height: 100vh;
    width: 100%;
    max-width: 1800px; /* Increased width for larger images */
    margin: 0 auto;
    overflow: hidden;
  }

  /* Each slide */
  .mySlides {
    position: absolute;
    width: 100%;
    height: 100%;
    background-size: cover;
    background-position: center;
    opacity: 0;
    transition: opacity 1.5s ease-in-out;
  }

  /* Make the first slide visible */
  .mySlides:first-child {
    opacity: 1;
  }
</style>

<script>
  let slideIndex = 0;
  const slides = document.querySelectorAll('.mySlides');
  
  function showSlides() {
    slides.forEach((slide) => {
      slide.style.opacity = '0';  // Hide all slides
    });
    slideIndex++;
    if (slideIndex > slides.length) { slideIndex = 1; }  // Loop back to first slide
    slides[slideIndex - 1].style.opacity = '1';  // Show the current slide
    setTimeout(showSlides, 3000);  // Change image every 3 seconds
  }

  document.addEventListener("DOMContentLoaded", function() {
    showSlides();  // Initialize the slideshow
  });
</script>
